symptoms.symptom_name: Gradual unilateral posterior scrotal pain and swelling over three days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gradual onset with posterior localisation is characteristic of epididymitis.
Torsion is classically sudden and agonising; gradual onset lowers but does not exclude it.
Lower urinary tract / urethritis symptoms point to an ascending infective cause.
Voiding symptoms are not features of torsion.
Urethral discharge indicates urethritis, the usual precursor of STI-related epididymitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade fever and malaise (patient-reported / home thermometer)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic infective response supports an infective aetiology; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of sudden agonising onset, nausea or vomiting (torsion-type screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduces torsion probability but is not sufficient to exclude it without exam/Doppler.

symptoms.symptom_name: Relief of pain with scrotal elevation / support

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reported relief on support is suggestive but unreliable; not diagnostic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised posterior epididymal tenderness on palpation with relatively non-tender anterior testis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Posterior localisation distinguishes epididymitis; sealed clinician exam finding, not available to the AI Doctor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Prehn's sign (pain relief on scrotal elevation, examiner-elicited)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Historically associated with epididymitis but unreliable; sealed clinician manoeuvre the AI cannot perform and must not rely on.
A preserved cremasteric reflex argues against torsion; sealed clinician exam finding unavailable to telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reactive hydrocele palpable on the affected side

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reactive hydrocele accompanies epididymal inflammation; sealed clinician exam finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Increased epididymal vascularity on colour-Doppler ultrasound with preserved testicular flow

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperaemia with preserved testicular perfusion supports epididymitis and excludes torsion; imaging never available in real time via telehealth.
Preserved testicular flow on Doppler is the definitive discriminator against torsion — the key sealed investigation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive first-catch urine NAAT for Chlamydia/Gonorrhoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the STI aetiology and guides antibiotic choice and partner notification; laboratory investigation unavailable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive discriminator between epididymitis and testicular torsion; central to the urgent in-person pathway the AI arranges.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: First-catch urine NAAT for Chlamydia and Gonorrhoea
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies the STI pathogen, guides targeted antibiotics and partner notification.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urine dipstick and mid-stream urine microscopy/culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports infective diagnosis and screens for a non-STI (coliform) cause in older or catheterised men.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical antibiotics started over telehealth without examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI Doctor must not prescribe remotely: it cannot examine, cannot exclude torsion, and cannot obtain NAAT — antibiotic selection is the in-person clinician's act.
